title: Grastidium summerhayesianum
taxonomy: Grastidium summerhayesianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It is a part of the genus Grastidium, which consists of several species of grasses. Grastidium summerhayesianum is native to Australia and is often found in dry and open woodland areas. The plant is a small, perennial species that produces small, yellowish flowers. As a member of the Poaceae family, it is closely related to other grasses such as oats and wheat.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various skin conditions and infections.
